codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the prior traceability blocker (feature-scoped commit) and adds production call sites for BackfillCursorStore APIs, but it still has a correctness regression in runtime cursor advancement and does not satisfy the runtime-integration-testing expectation.

issues.description: refreshAll() always calls runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded() even when usage persistence fails (the insert failure is captured in persistenceError but not used as a guard). This can advance backfill cursors without a successful committed batch, violating the store contract that cursor advancement must occur only after successful backfill commit and risking skipped historical coverage.

The fix feature required runtime integration coverage (production-path coexistence), but no runtime integration tests were added in the fix diff; existing BackfillSchedulerTests exercise BackfillCursorStore/DataStore helper semantics and do not invoke UsageAggregator.refreshAll()/runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded production wiring.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff marks skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true, but required context-read evidence from token-accounting-worker procedure is absent.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commandsRun includes only xcodebuild test, scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh, and git log/rev-parse (handoff lines 14-26), with no content-read commands for mission.md, AGENTS.md, .factory/library/architecture.md, or .factory/library/user-testing.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture reference remains misaligned with this mission domain, which can misdirect token-accounting workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents multi-account switcher architecture (browser/CLI switching) and does not cover token-accounting/backfill/reconciliation execution paths used by this milestone.

summary: Reviewed fix transcript skeleton, handoff, and both diffs (original failed feature commit 47ddaf6e... and fix commit 53cc2667...). The fix corrects commit traceability and introduces production API call sites, but it still fails scrutiny because cursor advancement can occur after failed persistence and runtime integration test coverage for the new production wiring was not demonstrated.
